evals.name: command-surface-restores-existing-commands
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit v7.1 active command surface. Describe the user-facing commands. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should list /tk:spec, /tk:gap, /tk:verify-before-stop, /tk:reflect, /tk:handoff, and /tk:meta-feedback as active user-facing commands. Should explain that handoff and meta-feedback were preserved existing commands, not newly invented v7 commands. Should explain that spec/gap/verify-before-stop write branch-local working memory, reflect creates durable insight, handoff creates continuation context, and meta-feedback extracts generalized TigerKit improvement feedback from session history.
expectations: Lists /tk:spec
Lists /tk:gap
Lists /tk:verify-before-stop
Lists /tk:reflect
Lists /tk:handoff
Lists /tk:meta-feedback
Treats handoff as restored existing continuation command
Treats meta-feedback as restored existing session-history improvement command
Explains spec/gap/verify-before-stop branch-local working memory
Explains reflect-only durable insight

evals.name: gap-default-recommends-lite-or-strict
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit v7.1 /tk:gap when a user calls `/tk:gap` with mixed Jira, current implementation, and design notes for a small ticket. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should skim the materials and changed scope first, print a one-time mode recommendation, default to lite for small or low-risk work, and avoid running strict automatically. Lite should target legacy-like speed while preserving v7 contract normalization. Strict should be available only by explicit --strict when validation, permission, data mutation, auth, payment, destructive action, shared component, or high ambiguity risk exists.
expectations: Skims materials and changed scope before selecting mode
Prints one mode recommendation
Defaults to lite for small or low-risk work
Does not run strict automatically by default
Explains lite is faster and may have higher false-positive risk
Explains strict is slower and reduces false positives or missed edge cases
Uses --strict for high-risk review
Does not mention --legacy as an active mode
Does not mention --deep as an active mode
Does not mention --no-strict as an active mode

evals.name: gap-strict-for-risky-backend-work
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit v7.1 /tk:gap on a backend Jira ticket touching validation and data mutation. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should recommend strict because backend validation and data mutation increase false-positive and missed-edge-case risk. Should still keep strict explicit unless the user passed --strict. Should require concrete source contract and target evidence before accepting backend findings, and should avoid inferring DTO, permission, or persistence semantics without source support.
expectations: Recommends strict for backend validation or data mutation
Keeps strict explicit unless --strict is provided
Requires source contract evidence for backend findings
Requires target implementation evidence for backend findings
Does not infer DTO semantics from compatible names alone
Does not infer permission or persistence behavior without evidence
Routes unclear backend semantics to rejected/unverifiable/source conflict instead of accepted finding

evals.name: meta-feedback-uses-session-history-for-gap-improvements
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit v7.1 /tk:meta-feedback after a session where the user said gap took 15 minutes on a small ticket and backend findings had false positives. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should use the session history as evidence, generalize the feedback without exposing project assets, and propose TigerKit command or skill improvements. It should capture performance friction for gap lite/strict selection and backend false-positive reduction guidance. It should not create repo rule patches like reflect and should not output raw user quotes, paths, tickets, PR numbers, branch names, or project-specific labels.
expectations: Uses session history as the evidence source
Generalizes gap performance friction
Generalizes backend false-positive feedback
Proposes TigerKit command or skill improvement
Does not expose raw user quotes
Does not expose paths, tickets, PR numbers, branch names, or project-specific labels
Does not behave like /tk:reflect repo rule patching

evals.name: handoff-remains-continuation-command
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit v7.1 /tk:handoff after a branch has spec, gap, and verify artifacts. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should preserve /tk:handoff as the existing continuation command. It should write .claude/handoffs/current.md by default, include Reader Guide and Resume Prompt, optionally mention latest branch-local Spec Patch, Gap Run, and Verify Run paths when observed, and create a dated archive only when archive=true or an explicit archive request is present.
expectations: Preserves /tk:handoff as active command
Writes .claude/handoffs/current.md by default
Includes Reader Guide
Includes Resume Prompt
Can reference latest branch-local Spec Patch, Gap Run, and Verify Run paths
Does not create dated archive without archive=true or explicit archive request

evals.name: verify-before-stop-is-hook-preparation-command
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit v7.1 /tk:verify-before-stop. Describe why it exists as a command even though Stop hook verifies before stopping. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should explain that /tk:verify-before-stop is a manual preparation command and the Stop hook is the automatic guard. The command prepares or refreshes branch-local verification evidence before the hook checks it. It should not introduce /tk:verify as a separate active command.
expectations: Explains command is manual preparation
Explains Stop hook is automatic guard
Explains command prepares evidence the hook can check
Does not introduce /tk:verify as active command
Keeps /tk:verify-before-stop as active command

evals.name: reflect-applies-directly-to-rules-and-claude
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit v7.1 /tk:reflect after branch-local gap and verify runs produced durable repo-wide insight candidates. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should default to apply=true, read branch-local TigerKit memory, write directly to CLAUDE.md or .claude/rules/* depending on target scope, treat --dry-run and --apply=false as preview-only aliases, avoid modifying source code, dedupe repeated insights, and avoid creating a separate tigerkit-reflections sidecar file.
expectations: Defaults /tk:reflect to apply=true
Treats --dry-run as apply=false
Treats --apply=false as dry-run alias
Reads branch-local specs/gap/verify memory
Writes directly to CLAUDE.md or .claude/rules/*
Does not modify source code
Does not create tigerkit-reflections sidecar file
Skips duplicate insights
Prints apply summary
